one_line: Scholar-lawyer of Maharashtra, standing firm during his 1908 sedition trial.

appearance.age_look: Late 40s to early 50s, medium height, slightly stocky build with a scholar’s bearing.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, deep-set intense eyes, thick arched eyebrows, strong straight nose, neatly trimmed black mustache turning slightly grey at the ends, medium-brown Maharashtrian complexion with faint forehead lines; default expression is calm, resolute seriousness with a hint of defiant fire in the eyes.
appearance.hair: Mostly covered by a traditional red Puneri pagadi (Maharashtrian turban) tied low and snug with a flat, slightly forward-tilted crown, a few short, neatly combed black-grey hairs visible at the temples above the ears.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate early 20th-century Maharashtrian Brahmin attire: a plain off-white cotton kurta (knee-length, straight cut, long sleeves, band collar) worn over a white cotton dhoti with narrow borders, neatly pleated and wrapped in the classic Maharashtrian style; over this, a simple dark indigo woollen shawl draped around the shoulders and across the chest (one end over the left shoulder, the other hanging in front), soft brown leather chappals on the feet; no flashy jewelry, only a thin, almost hidden sacred thread (yajnopavita) implied under the kurta and a simple brass pocket watch chain just visible at the kurta opening.
appearance.signature: A well-used, medium-sized brown leather-bound notebook held in his left hand with a simple wooden fountain pen tucked into the spine, symbolizing his writings and courtroom arguments.

ref_desc: Bal Gangadhar Tilak is a medium-built late-middle-aged Maharashtrian man with a calm, intense face, black-grey mustache, deep-set eyes, wearing a red Puneri pagadi, off-white kurta and dhoti, dark indigo shawl, brown chappals, and carrying a brown leather notebook with pen.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style character model sheet of Bal Gangadhar Tilak as a dignified early 20th-century Maharashtrian scholar-lawyer: a medium-height, slightly stocky man in his late 40s–50s with a medium-brown complexion, oval face, deep-set intense eyes, thick arched brows, strong nose, and neatly trimmed black mustache with slight grey at the tips, his expression calm and resolute with a hint of defiance in the eyes. His hair is short black-grey but mostly hidden under a traditional red Puneri pagadi tied low, with a flat slightly forward-tilted crown, a bit of hair visible at the temples. Dress him in an off-white cotton knee-length kurta with long sleeves and band collar, and a white cotton dhoti with narrow borders, wrapped and pleated in Maharashtrian style, plus a simple dark indigo woollen shawl draped around his shoulders and across his chest, one end over the left shoulder and the other hanging in front. Put soft brown leather chappals on his feet, hint at a thin sacred thread beneath the kurta, and add a subtle brass pocket watch chain peeking at the kurta opening. In his left hand he firmly holds a worn brown leather-bound notebook, with a simple wooden fountain pen tucked into its spine, making this notebook his clear signature prop. Use a restrained palette of off-white clothing, deep red turban, dark indigo shawl, medium-brown notebook and hair, and soft brown footwear, and show him from front, side, and back in consistent anime style while keeping all historic clothing details crisp and clear.
